sections.title: What is Reported Speech?
points: It tells what someone else said without using their exact words.
We usually change the verb tense back in time.
Example: 'I am happy' becomes 'He said he was happy.'

sections.title: Common Changes
points: Will becomes would, can becomes could.

sections.text: If the reporting verb is in the past, the reported verb usually moves back one tense.

cards.fact: When someone’s exact words are shown in quotes.
When we describe what someone said using a reporting verb.
cards.mnemonic: He said that he was going.

cards.fact: A change in verb tense from direct to reported speech.
